steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: A 193% increase means multiply by $1 + 1.93 = 2.93$. A 31% decrease means multiply by $1 - 0.31 = 0.69$.

steps.title: Chain the multipliers across both years
steps.content: Starting with value $V$ at end 2016, the toy's value is multiplied by 2.93 in 2017, then by 0.69 in 2018: $$V \times 2.93 \times 0.69 = V \times (2.93 \times 0.69) = V \times 2.0217$$

steps.title: Calculate the net percentage increase
steps.content: The value changed from $V$ to $2.0217V$, a factor of 2.0217. The net percentage increase is $(2.0217 - 1) \times 100\% = 1.0217 \times 100\% = 102.17\%$. A is correct.
